meta.title: Rezics | inherited · create · spread
meta.description: Rezics is a community for works, knowledge, and creativity to be inherited, created anew, and spread onward across platforms, languages, and communities.

sections.title: Web novels and born-digital books have already outgrown old catalogs.
sections.body: Serialized fiction, independent writing, and platform-native books move too quickly and across too many sites for legacy shelves and single-language databases to organize them well.

sections.title: As creation gets easier, every medium will need a better public index.
sections.body: The same pressure is coming for games, media, posts, worlds, and mixed forms of work. Rezics is built for that future from the start.

storySections.title: People need one place to find the work, not just one platform's copy of it.
storySections.body: A work can appear through many publishers, languages, aliases, editions, and communities. Rezics gives it one shared identity so readers can cross those boundaries and still arrive at the same context.

storySections.title: Tags and shelves should help people find what actually fits them.
storySections.body: Rezics combines shared tags, realm-scoped classification, and shelves that can carry reviews. Browsing can show both what a work is and why someone thought it belonged there.

storySections.title: Better classification helps creators meet the right readers.
storySections.body: When readers can describe works in community language, creators get clearer signals about who a work reaches, where it resonates, and which nearby audiences may care.

storySections.title: Communities should not have to split the catalog, discussion, and wiki.
storySections.body: Realms gather people around what they love and let them classify, discuss, curate, and document works beside the shared public index instead of scattering context across separate tools.
